mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at a low level (1.95%) signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supporting positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as rates stay low.
evidence_refs: fact:Low policy rates historically correlate with rising equity valuations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 3% but 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 3.7% is below typical natural rate estimates, signaling a very tight labor market, which indicates late-cycle conditions, eroding corporate margins through wage pressures and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% and is lower than 3 months prior
evidence_refs: fact:Late-cycle negative equity returns occurred in 2000 and 2007 when UNRATE was near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE below NAIRU estimate of 4.5%)
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops further but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered months, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with steady or falling unemployment signals robust economic expansion with sufficient supply capacity, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% and UNRATE is unchanged or falling
evidence_refs: fact:ISM manufacturing expansion coincident with strong equity returns
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: With CPIAUCSL above 250 (a level indicating inflation above the Federal Reserve's target) and the FEDFUNDS rate rising, the regime of high inflation triggers monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL greater than 250 and FEDFUNDS higher than the previous month
evidence_refs: fact:1970s inflation tightening episodes produced negative equity returns
regime_hint: high inflation regime (CPI > 250) with monetary tightening
failure_condition: If in this regime 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or if FEDFUNDS decreases while CPIAUCSL remains above 250, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, consistently driving positive S&P 500 returns over the short term; this always-on trend signal forecasts positive weekly returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment growth strongly positively correlated with equity bull markets
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months and 5-day forward returns are not negative on average, or if positive PAYEMS growth coincides with negative returns more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth above a 5% year-over-year threshold for two consecutive months signals overheating consumer demand that prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, ultimately slowing the economy and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds 5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales spikes preceded equity market peaks in 2000 and 2007
failure_condition: If after the trigger S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with an increasing federal funds rate indicates a booming housing sector met with monetary tightening, which historically leads to higher mortgage rates, falling housing demand, and eventual negative spillover to equities, predicting a drop over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month increase exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS has increased in the previous 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts often peak before equity downturns when rates rise
regime_hint: housing market expansion under tightening cycle
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of an extremely tight labor market (UNRATE <= 4.0%) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase exceeding 0.2%) signals wage-driven inflation that forces aggressive monetary policy tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 4.0% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month percentage change > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:Phillips curve dynamics historically preceded recessions and equity downturns
regime_hint: late cycle with tight labor and rising inflation
failure_condition: If after the trigger 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or if inflation subsequently declines without a market drop, the hypothesis fails.
